title: got() with https.rejectUnauthorized: false — the HTTP client skips TLS certificate validation (MITM)

params.passDetail: got keeps certificate validation on (https.rejectUnauthorized is not false) — the server's TLS identity is verified.
params.failDetail: got is called with https: { rejectUnauthorized: false }, disabling TLS certificate validation for the request. Any machine on the network path can present a forged certificate and man-in-the-middle the call — capturing credentials, tokens, and response data. Remove rejectUnauthorized: false; pass a trusted CA via https.certificateAuthority if a private CA is required.
params.absentCallDetail: No got() call in this scope; this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: got() does not set https.rejectUnauthorized; the secure default (validate) applies.

provenance.note: https: { rejectUnauthorized: false } is pasted to get past a self-signed cert during integration and left in the shared client, silently disabling MITM protection for every request it makes.
